nodes.title: Radiation Science
nodes.description: Provides a broad based understanding of the fundamentals of radiation science including the ways in which radiation is produced and utilized, the principles of radioactive decay, radiation exposure, absorbed dose, shielding and detection of radiation. Prerequisite: MATH 1212 or MATH 1234. Co-requisites: RADT 2520 or Instructor permission for non-majors.
nodes.other_requirements: Co-requisites: RADT 2520 or Instructor permission for non-majors. Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Techniques Radiation Therapy
nodes.description: Advanced theory and clinical application of radiotherapeutic techniques. Co-requisites: RADT 3850, RADT 3780, Radiation Therapy major.
nodes.other_requirements: Radiation Therapy major. Senior MRS and RADT students only; Concurrent enrollment in RADT 3850; Lab Fee: $100.00. Class will be held in Rowell 104

nodes.title: Clincal Practicum IV
nodes.description: RADT students are assigned to approved clinical education sites to observe and increase their participation in the clinical environment. Evaluations based on defined clinical objectives and competencies to be completed by the clinical and University faculty. Spring. Prerequisites: Successful completion of all previous required major courses and concurrent enrollment in RADT 3890.
nodes.other_requirements: Successful completion of all previous required major courses; Senior standing only; Safety Badge fee: $59.00; Contact instructor for location

nodes.title: Elementary Physics II
nodes.description: Algebra-based survey of electricity, magnetism, optics and modern physics. Appropriate for students in health and life sciences. Counts as a lecture and lab. Optional accompanying problem solving recitation: PHYS 1460. Prerequisites: PHYS 1400, PHYS 1500, or PHYS 1600.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for labs L01 - L03; Open to Degree and PACE Students

nodes.title: Intro to Clinical Practice
nodes.description: Introduction to the clinical environment through activities which include patient care issues, treatment unit operations and manipulations and direct patient case. Includes a clinical practicum. Pre-requisite: RADT 2520.
nodes.other_requirements: MRS and RADT majors ONLY; Radiation Safety Badge Fee: $28; Class will be held in Rowell 104; PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: Ugr Hum Anatomy & Physiology 2
nodes.description: Part II of two-semester course sequence. Structure and function of human body. Prerequisite: ANPS 1190.
nodes.other_requirements: Common hour exams are held 5:30-7:00pm on these Mondays: 2/9, 3/2, and 4/6. BIOM,CSD,DIET,DNFS,EXSC,HSCI,MLS,NFS, PE, PHS, MRS, PRNU; Majors only until Dec. 2nd, Please contact Patrick Mullen (patrick.c.mullen@uvm.edu) with any questions; Class meets 3x a week with online lab comprising 4th credit; Degree students only even after level restrictions removed; PACE students register for ANPS 1200 ZRA; Prereq enforced by the system:ANPS 1190.

nodes.title: CT Procedures
nodes.description: Provides in-depth study of the concepts, use and practice of CT Procedures related to Nuclear Medicine Technology and Radiation Therapy. Prerequisites: ANPS 1190, ANPS 1200, BHSC 2750.
nodes.other_requirements: RADT and MRS majors only; Others by instructor permission; Open to Degree and PACE students; Asynchronous online

nodes.title: Intro to Human Disease
nodes.description: Elementary course in human pathology designed for Allied Health students. First portion deals with general mechanisms of disease, followed by disorders of specific organs. Prerequisites: ANPS 1190 and ANPS 1200.
nodes.other_requirements: MLS, MRS, NMT and RADT majors only; Prerequisites: College biology and two successfully completed semesters of Anatomy and Physiology.

nodes.title: Essentials of Patient Care
nodes.description: Presents all aspects of care associated with the treatment of cancer when patients receive Radiation Therapy. Prerequisites: RADT 2520, RADT 2850.
nodes.other_requirements: RADT and MRS majors only

nodes.title: Cross Sectional Imaging
nodes.description: Introduction to the radiographic anatomy and the various imaging modalities presently used to include diagnostic imaging, computed tomography (CT), magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), and nuclear medicine. Prerequisites: ANPS 1200.
nodes.other_requirements: other degree students with instructor permission

nodes.title: Dosimetry
nodes.description: Treatment plan verification using three-dimensional computer models, simulation data, and knowledge of treatment unit capabilities. Prerequisites: RADT 3700, Radiation Therapy major.
nodes.other_requirements: Radiation Therapy major; RADT and MRS majors only; Class will be held in Rowell 104

nodes.title: Dosimetry Concepts
nodes.description: Introduces dosimetry, treatment planning and quality assurance concepts to prepare for clinical dosimetry rotations. Prerequisites: BHSC 2400; PHYS 1250 or PHYS 1400 and PHYS 1450.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override. Prereqs enforced by the system: or instructor permission; MRS and RADT students only; Minimum Junior standing; Will be held In Rowell 104

nodes.title: Qual Assurance&Treatment Plan
nodes.description: The integration of clinical oncology, radiobiology, dosimetry, and treatment planning, and how they affect patient outcomes. Prerequisites: RADT 3850, RADT 3770, RADT 3780; Senior standing. Co-requisite: RADT 3870.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing

nodes.title: Prin of Radiation Therapy
nodes.description: Introduction to the practice and theory of radiation therapy. Co-requisite: BHSC 2400 or Instructor permission for non-majors.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission for non-majors, RADT, MRS majors only, or instructor permission. Open to Degree and PACE students.

nodes.title: Ugr Hum Anatomy & Physiology 1
nodes.description: Part I of two-semester course sequence. Structure and function of human body.
nodes.other_requirements: Prereg limited to: BMCS, BIOM, CSD, EMS, EXSC, HSCI, MLS, MRS, NFS, NMT, PE, PHS, PRNU majors only. Degree students only even after major restrictions lifted.

nodes.title: Elementary Physics I
nodes.description: Algebra-based survey of mechanics, oscillations, waves and thermal physics. Appropriate for students in health and life sciences. Counts as lecture and lab. Optional accompanying problem solving recitation section: PHYS 1410. Prerequisites: High school algebra and trigonometry.
nodes.other_requirements: Must register for PHYS 1400 L01-L04; Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Senior Seminar in Rad Therapy
nodes.description: Evaluate current trends in advanced treatment techniques with the premise of clinical research and modern technology used in oncology. Helps prepare students for the American Registry of Radiologic Technologists national certification exam. Prerequisites: RADT 3440, RADT 3710. Co-requisites: RADT 3850, RADT 3770.
nodes.other_requirements: MRS students only; Synchronous online course component; Class will meet in-person M 3:30 p.m.- 4:20 p.m.;

nodes.title: Clinical Practicum III
nodes.description: A continuation of RADT 2870 emphasizing increasing clinical capabilities. Prerequisite: RADT 2870. Co-requisite: RADT 3770.
nodes.other_requirements: MRS and RADT majors only; Held at UVMMC as Clinical Practicum; Radiation Safety Badge Fee: $23

nodes.title: Conceptual Physics
nodes.description: One-semester conceptual survey. Topics selected from mechanics, electricity, magnetism and modern physics.

nodes.title: Clinical Radiation Oncology
nodes.description: The various types of neoplasms, methods of diagnosis of treatment, and elementary pathology are presented. Prerequisites: ANPS 1190, ANPS 1200, PATH 2010, Radiation Therapy majors. Co-requisite: RADT 2870 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Radiation Therapy majors. RADT, MRS majors only

nodes.title: Final Clinical Pract Overview
nodes.description: To orient the student to a new radiation oncology department; understand basic patient flow and essential equipment. The student is also responsible for completing all necessary orientation requirements at the organization, department level, or both. This includes understanding relevant policies and procedures and SOP's. Prerequisites: RADT 3710, RADT 2760, RADT 2870, RADT 3440; Senior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing; Immunization Tracking fee: $25; HIPAA/OSHA fee: $14

nodes.title: Clinical Practicum II
nodes.description: Students participate and observe in the University of Vermont Medical Center Radiation Therapy Department. Prerequisite: RADT 2850.
nodes.other_requirements:
Immunization Tracking Fee: $25
HIPAA/OSHA Fee: $14
